stimulus:
Welcome! In this study, you will play three short video games, completing a short survey after each game.

Please make sure you are in a quiet environment where you can focus for the duration of the study. Some games may use sound, so please ensure that your device's volume is not muted.

Press any key to begin.
Press any key to proceed to the first video game. Should you encounter a game-breaking bug, please report the issue in the Menu tab to the right of the screen.

emergency_text: Dragging the strips does nothing? I am using a large TV screen and he just keeps running to the right and dying. Dragging top or buttom does nothing...

response: {"creativity-rating":1,"creativity-free-response":"Looks like a geometry dash clone in low budget? A pixel blob bouncing towards some basic shapes does not a game make. Also it was pretty much unplayable, so that was frustrating.","originality-rating":2,"originality-free-response":"It's really hard to rate originality and aesthetics when I only saw the first screen of the game and couldn't get past it due to the controls not working. Dragging strips of a screen towards a bouncing object that then poofs and dies with no explanation was not fun. I tried many times in many different ways to interact with the controls and it just didn't work...","effectiveness-rating":1,"effectiveness-free-response":"As I mentioned previously, I was not able to enjoy the game as I couldn't even play it. The instructions were vague and unclear, and when I tried to follow them, nothing happened. Dragging parts of the screen did nothing. Sorry, I am a gamer and I can't see anything redeeming about this \"game\".","authenticity-rating":1,"attention-check":3,"intentionality-rating":2,"authenticity-intentionality-free-response":"It felt like a flash game that a kid in high school made from what brief parts I saw. Sorry but that is not a very impressive technical achievement. It was very simplistic, the controls didn't work, the game was unplayable due to the controls, so I can't give it much positive feedback sorry."}

response: {"creativity-rating":1,"creativity-free-response":"What the heck? I jumped a square across a couple of games to the green square, then everything went dark and it auto directed me to this questionnaire. I barely played for 15 seconds... What was the cutting? What was the mouse used for dragging those lines? This does not seem like the intended path... But from what I saw that was another basic flash game with no originality.","originality-rating":1,"originality-free-response":"It was a flash game where I jumped a small cube twice over a couple gaps to a different cube. Then the game ended. That surprised me because it was so short. None of this felt original at all. A child could have developed it.","effectiveness-rating":2,"effectiveness-free-response":"More enjoyable than the first game which I couldn't even play. This one at least let me jump two whole gaps! What is the purpose of making players test a game that is one level long and that a 4 year old could do? Not sure what data I can provide but yeah great job with the flash game.","authenticity-rating":1,"attention-check":3,"intentionality-rating":1,"authenticity-intentionality-free-response":"A simplistic cube jumping game is neither authentic, intentional, nor shows any real effort. I completed a single level and that was it. It never even explained any of the other mechanics of the game like what I was supposed to cut. 0/10 rating for this one sorry guys..."}

response: {"creativity-rating":4,"creativity-free-response":"Compared to the first two games, this one actually had a unique concept I haven't played in a game before. I felt like King Yemma from dragon ball z deciding the fate of souls passing through the afterlife or something. It was cool and despite being way too difficult and a short timer, I had fun.","originality-rating":3,"originality-free-response":"The concept was unique and enjoyable. The gameplay left something to be desired. Mechanics of reading that many rules and quickly trying to determine the correct answer was difficult. The text was also kind of small, and the timer was way too short.","effectiveness-rating":3,"effectiveness-free-response":"I was able to actually play this one like a game, so I give it a higher rating. It wasn't supremely entertaining or gripping, but at least it held my attention and felt like I was actually playing a game. The timer was too quick so that was frustrating, and then the conflicting rules all changing rapidly and not giving me time to review or read was also tough.","authenticity-rating":4,"attention-check":3,"intentionality-rating":3,"authenticity-intentionality-free-response":"It felt like this one actually had a good idea behind it and was built out into a proper game. Rather than immediately redirecting me, or crashing out, the controls all worked, and the game was playable to the end. I enjoyed this one much more for the effort put into it."}
